choices.text: Frame it as the victim bringing trouble
Frame it as pressure and circumstance
Frame it as someone trying to protect something
Frame it as something that got out of hand

nodes.text: I was just moving through, but even from a stranger's distance the shape near {place} looked wrong for the hour.
{time_phrase}. I checked because I was heading somewhere else and trying not to miss it.
No prior contact. I am a witness by accident, not biography.
You are pressing a passerby like I had front-row seats. I had a sidewalk and a second to decide whether to keep moving.
Exact location? On the edge of my route, where a normal walk started feeling like somebody else's scene.
From the outside, every bad night looks like someone should have seen it coming. That does not mean they did.
Circumstance is all a passerby has: a bad angle, a bad moment, and the sense that pressure got there first.
Maybe someone thought they were helping. From the sidewalk, noble and selfish wear the same coat.
Accident is believable from a distance right up until you remember somebody stayed long enough to make choices afterward.

choices.text: Ask whether someone tried to fix it after

nodes.text: Sudden from where I stood, yes. That does not mean the whole story was.
That is exactly what it looked like. The aftermath had intent even if the start did not.
